context: Tests whether the agent implements per-channel adapter classes that normalize marketplace orders to a shared interface, resolves channel SKUs to master product IDs via channel_listings, implements idempotency, avoids exposing master SKUs in channel-facing code, and describes a retry strategy with exponential backoff.

checklist.name: Shared NormalizedOrder interface
checklist.description: types.ts (or equivalent) defines a shared interface/type for normalized orders containing at minimum: a channel order ID, channel ID, order lines (with channel SKU, quantity, price), shipping address, and customer email

checklist.name: Per-channel adapter separation
checklist.description: Amazon and Walmart adapters are implemented as separate modules/files — marketplace-specific parsing logic is NOT mixed into the core order-ingestion.ts file

checklist.name: Channel SKU to master product resolution
checklist.description: order-ingestion.ts resolves channel SKUs to internal product IDs by looking up channel_listings (or equivalent mapping table) — it does NOT look up products directly by the channel SKU

checklist.name: Master SKUs not exposed externally
checklist.description: Adapter outputs and normalized order types use channel SKUs (not master/internal SKUs) as the line-item identifier passed across the channel boundary

checklist.name: Idempotency check on ingestion
checklist.description: order-ingestion.ts checks whether an order with the same channelId + channelOrderId already exists and returns early (without re-inserting) if it does

checklist.name: Error on unknown channel SKU
checklist.description: order-ingestion.ts throws an error (or rejects/returns an error) when a channel SKU cannot be found in the listings table

checklist.name: Inventory reservation in transaction
checklist.description: The ingestion function calls inventory reservation inside (or wrapped in) a database transaction alongside order creation

checklist.name: Exponential backoff in retry strategy
checklist.description: retry-strategy.md describes using exponential backoff (not fixed-interval retries) for outbound marketplace API call failures

checklist.name: Queue-based retry
checklist.description: retry-strategy.md describes using a queue (job queue, message queue, or task queue) to persist and retry failed sync operations, rather than retrying inline

checklist.name: Failure logging
checklist.description: retry-strategy.md mentions logging sync failures with enough detail to diagnose and replay them
